llm_judge_candidates.truth_value: Hydrogen embrittlement study on Fe-26Mn-11Al-1.2C: tensile ductility drastically reduced under hydrogen charging; two damage mechanisms identified: grain boundary triple junction cracking and void chains along grain boundaries due to planar slip localization. Thermal desorption analysis indicates κ-carbides act as hydrogen trap sites with activation energy ~76–80 kJ/mol (Redlich–Kister analysis). (Fig. 8, 9)
llm_judge_candidates.prediction_value: Hydrogen embrittlement in Fe-26Mn-11Al-1.2C involves grain boundary triple junction cracking and microvoid chain formation along grain boundaries linked to planar slip localization induced by kappa-carbides.

(fig. 8, 9)
